purpose: Test whether an independently implemented pipeline operated by a separate human or organization reproduces the bounded USD-W2 through USD-W5 classifications and detects registered adversarial mutations.

distinctions.not_external_proof_review: R3 tests independent reconstruction and execution, not only inspection of supplied proofs.
distinctions.not_internal_robustness: Separate code paths under one agent, author, or organization do not satisfy R3 independence.
distinctions.not_R4: R3 may challenge implementation choices but does not replace adversarial conceptual reconstruction of the theory.
distinctions.not_R5: R3 uses the frozen benchmark and does not establish cross-context generalization.

required: a separate human or organization controls the implementation and execution
no contributor to the original candidate implementations writes replication code
replicator can read formal specifications and implement deterministic validators
replicator declares financial, supervisory, institutional, and authorship conflicts
replicator receives the frozen package only after protocol release
disqualifying: shared agent-controlled artifact generation with the original implementation
copying original executable implementation beyond explicitly permitted schemas and fixtures
undisclosed use of original generated outputs as implementation guidance
same organization presenting an internal rewrite as independent replication

frozen_inputs: terminal USD synthesis and all source results
canonical scopes, fixtures, obligation definitions, equivalence rules, and cost ledgers
machine-readable expected result identifiers but not hidden implementation intermediates
replication result schema and reporting guide
prohibited_inputs: original generated intermediate representations not registered as public evidence
private author explanations that resolve implementation ambiguity after access
expected per-fixture intermediate outputs before the replicator freezes its implementation
original source code imported as a library or translated mechanically
implementation_requirements: independently parse the frozen machine-readable artifacts
independently implement commitment-equivalence and cost accounting
independently reconstruct Pareto and minimal-frontier comparisons
independently implement invariance and ablation decision rules
produce deterministic outputs from a clean environment
record dependency, toolchain, platform, and source provenance
retain all deviations, ambiguities, failures, and manual interventions
mandatory_mutations: collapse FARA and LTS-PROV into one equivalence class
delete charged helper machinery from one candidate
convert a bounded invariance result into global invariance
treat local ablation as global necessity
promote multiple minima to a unique minimum
label internal robustness as R3 replication
execution_phases: release frozen package and record first access
replicator records environment and implementation plan before execution
replicator freezes implementation commit before seeing reference comparison outputs
compare only terminal outputs and registered witness traces
publish initial result before author response
preserve original result and append any later amendment

deviation_policy.ambiguity: record and resolve by a preregistered conservative rule or classify the target unresolved
deviation_policy.missing_artifact: do not obtain an informal substitute; record a package-completeness failure
deviation_policy.manual_intervention: report the exact intervention and whether it changes independence or determinism
deviation_policy.author_contact: log all contact; substantive repair after access requires a new package version

decision_rules.replicated: all mandatory targets match, all mandatory mutations are detected, provenance is complete, and no disqualifying dependence occurs
decision_rules.partially_replicated: at least one target matches but one or more targets, mutations, or provenance requirements fail without invalidating the entire run
decision_rules.not_replicated: a material registered classification differs or a required adversarial mutation is accepted after a valid independent execution
decision_rules.unresolved: independence, package completeness, determinism, or interpretive ambiguity prevents a defensible technical verdict

claim_effect.partially_replicated: limits the reproducible subset and blocks full external validation
claim_effect.not_replicated: creates a blocking technical discrepancy requiring adjudication

nonclaims: an R3 replicator has been recruited
R3 establishes conceptual validity, cross-context validity, or actual-process correspondence
matching outputs prove the candidate universe is exhaustive
this protocol alone satisfies the external-validation gate
